title: Programming Fundamentals with BookWorm
description: Master the core concepts of programming through guided, adaptive learning
learning_objectives: Understand variables, data types, and memory concepts
Master control flow and logical reasoning

concepts.name: Variables and Data Types
concepts.description: Variables are named containers for storing values. Like labeled boxes, they hold different types of data (numbers, text, true/false) that your program can use and modify.

concepts.name: Operators and Expressions
concepts.description: Operators are symbols that perform operations on values. They let you do math (+, -, *, /), compare values (>, <, ==), and combine logical conditions (and, or, not).

concepts.name: Conditional Logic (if/else)
concepts.description: Conditionals let your program make decisions. Like a choose-your-own-adventure book, the code takes different paths based on whether conditions are true or false.

concepts.name: While Loops
concepts.description: Loops repeat actions until a condition is met. A while loop is like saying 'keep doing this as long as X is true' - useful for unknown repetition counts.

concepts.name: For Loops
concepts.description: For loops iterate over sequences. Like processing items on an assembly line one by one, they're perfect when you know exactly how many times to repeat.

concepts.name: Functions and Parameters
concepts.description: Functions are reusable blocks of code with a name. Like recipes, they take inputs (parameters), do something, and can return outputs. They keep code organized and DRY.

concepts.name: Lists and Arrays
concepts.description: Lists store multiple values in order. Like a shopping list, you can add items, remove them, access by position, and loop through all elements.

concepts.name: Dictionaries and Key-Value Pairs
concepts.description: Dictionaries store data as key-value pairs. Like a real dictionary where you look up words (keys) to find definitions (values), they enable fast lookups and structured data.

concepts.name: String Operations
concepts.description: Strings are text data. You can slice them (get parts), join them together, search within them, and transform them (uppercase, lowercase, replace).

concepts.name: Error Handling and Debugging
concepts.description: Errors happen. Good code anticipates them with try/except blocks. Debugging is detective work: read errors, add print statements, test assumptions, isolate problems.

concepts.name: Search Algorithms
concepts.description: Searching finds items in collections. Linear search checks each item. Binary search (on sorted data) divides and conquers for speed. Understanding Big O notation helps compare efficiency.

concepts.name: Sorting Algorithms
concepts.description: Sorting arranges data in order. Bubble sort swaps neighbors. Selection sort finds minimums. Merge sort divides and merges. Each has trade-offs in speed and memory.
